summary: High‑energy audience capture with crystal‑clear bass and a killer Jerry solo, but the tape runs slightly fast (~40 cents) making pitch correction desirable.
review: The recording is praised for its vivid, upfront sound and excellent bass response, delivering an engaging performance highlighted by Jerry Garcia’s second solo on “Iko Iko.” While most listeners love the clarity, a few note that the source runs about a semitone fast and could use a pitch fix. Overall the show is regarded as a strong outing from a great year, with the audio quality generally good despite the speed issue.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the set highly (5‑star) and praise both performance and sound, but a single low‑rating review points to serious source problems; combined with noted pitch‑speed issues the rating settles in the good‑to‑great range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, upfront sound with excellent bass and slight panning on "Iko Iko"; however the tape runs about a semitone fast (~40 cents) and would benefit from pitch correction.

show_quality.setlist_flow: No detailed comments on overall set structure; reviewers focus on the strength of the "Iko Iko" performance and overall vibe.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His second solo on "Iko Iko" is described as alive, kickin', and gives chills; vocals also praised.
